forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+title: Consolidate qwen + qwen-moe onto Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP and enable MTP
+---
+Goal: collapse the two Qwen model configs into one, pointed at the MTP build, with Multi-Token Prediction enabled for faster decode.
+
+Target model: https://huggingface.co/unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF/tree/main
+- repo: unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF
+- filename: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf
+
+Current state:
+- builtin/models/qwen.yaml → unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF / Qwen3.6-27B-UD-Q4_K_XL.gguf (dense)
+- builtin/models/qwen-moe.yaml → unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF / Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf (MoE, no MTP)
+
+Work:
+1. Point qwen.yaml at the MTP repo/file; remove qwen-moe.yaml (consolidate to one `qwen` model). Keep the `kanban` tag on qwen.
+2. Enable MTP. NEEDS RESEARCH: confirm whether the llama-cpp-2 fork (swissarmyhammer/llama-cpp-rs) + llama-cpp-sys-2 expose MTP / speculative MTP decode, and how it's turned on (context param vs separate API). If supported, add a ModelConfig field + plumb to context params (NO env vars — configure via model YAML, per project convention). If NOT supported, document the gap and scope the upstream/bindings work.
+3. Verify on Metal with a real-model test.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -8,14 +8,17 @@
 Goal: collapse the two Qwen model configs into one, pointed at the MTP build, with Multi-Token Prediction enabled for faster decode.

 Target model: https://huggingface.co/unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF/tree/main
-- repo: unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF
-- filename: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf
+- repo: unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF, filename: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf

-Current state:
-- builtin/models/qwen.yaml → unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF / Qwen3.6-27B-UD-Q4_K_XL.gguf (dense)
-- builtin/models/qwen-moe.yaml → unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF / Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf (MoE, no MTP)
+## RESEARCH FINDING (2026-05-29): MTP cannot be "enabled" via config in the current stack.
+Verified against the vendored llama.cpp (swissarmyhammer/llama-cpp-rs fork, llama.cpp snapshot ~2026-04, commit b8635075f):
+- The `qwen35moe` arch IS supported (src/models/qwen35moe.cpp) — the model loads & runs.
+- NextN/MTP support is LOAD-ONLY: the loader reads `nextn_predict_layers` and sets `n_layer_kv_from_start = n_layer - nextn_predict_layers`; the per-arch graph builders SKIP the NextN layer ("Only process up to last layer (skip final NextN layer)"). qwen35moe.cpp's graph does NOT reference nextn/mtp at all.
+- There is NO MTP self-speculative decode in the core path: no mtp/nextn in llama-context.cpp / llama-graph.cpp, and NO mtp/draft/speculative symbols in the public `include/llama.h` or the Rust bindings (llama-cpp-2 / llama-cpp-sys-2).
+- The only speculative decoding present is draft-model based `common/speculative.cpp` (example/server-level orchestration above the core API) — our `GenerationHelper` decode loop does not use it.
+
+Implication: pointing at the MTP-GGUF downloads larger files for ZERO runtime benefit today (the MTP head is loaded then skipped). Real MTP/speculative speedup requires implementing draft-model speculative decoding in our generation loop (+ possibly upstream MTP self-spec) — a substantial feature, not a flag.

-Work:
-1. Point qwen.yaml at the MTP repo/file; remove qwen-moe.yaml (consolidate to one `qwen` model). Keep the `kanban` tag on qwen.
-2. Enable MTP. NEEDS RESEARCH: confirm whether the llama-cpp-2 fork (swissarmyhammer/llama-cpp-rs) + llama-cpp-sys-2 expose MTP / speculative MTP decode, and how it's turned on (context param vs separate API). If supported, add a ModelConfig field + plumb to context params (NO env vars — configure via model YAML, per project convention). If NOT supported, document the gap and scope the upstream/bindings work.
-3. Verify on Metal with a real-model test.
\ No newline at end of file
+## Plan
+1. Consolidation (doable now): merge to a single `qwen` model (keep `kanban` tag), delete qwen-moe.yaml. Source choice pending user (MTP repo as requested = future-proof but inert/larger; vs regular Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF MXFP4_MOE = leaner, identical today).
+2. "Enable MTP": NOT possible via config. Filed as separate effort (speculative decoding implementation) — see follow-up.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,20 +5,33 @@
 position_ordinal: '80'
 title: Consolidate qwen + qwen-moe onto Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP and enable MTP
 ---
-Goal: collapse the two Qwen model configs into one, pointed at the MTP build, with Multi-Token Prediction enabled for faster decode.
+Goal: one qwen model on Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP + MTP (Multi-Token Prediction) speculative decode enabled.

-Target model: https://huggingface.co/unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF/tree/main
-- repo: unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF, filename: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf
+## DONE
+- Consolidated qwen + qwen-moe → single kanban-tagged `qwen` on unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF / Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf; deleted qwen-moe.yaml; fixed the 2 model-enumeration tests. Commit 305b63d46. Runs as a normal MoE today (MTP head carried but not yet driven).

-## RESEARCH FINDING (2026-05-29): MTP cannot be "enabled" via config in the current stack.
-Verified against the vendored llama.cpp (swissarmyhammer/llama-cpp-rs fork, llama.cpp snapshot ~2026-04, commit b8635075f):
-- The `qwen35moe` arch IS supported (src/models/qwen35moe.cpp) — the model loads & runs.
-- NextN/MTP support is LOAD-ONLY: the loader reads `nextn_predict_layers` and sets `n_layer_kv_from_start = n_layer - nextn_predict_layers`; the per-arch graph builders SKIP the NextN layer ("Only process up to last layer (skip final NextN layer)"). qwen35moe.cpp's graph does NOT reference nextn/mtp at all.
-- There is NO MTP self-speculative decode in the core path: no mtp/nextn in llama-context.cpp / llama-graph.cpp, and NO mtp/draft/speculative symbols in the public `include/llama.h` or the Rust bindings (llama-cpp-2 / llama-cpp-sys-2).
-- The only speculative decoding present is draft-model based `common/speculative.cpp` (example/server-level orchestration above the core API) — our `GenerationHelper` decode loop does not use it.
+## CORRECTED FINDING (initial reading was the STALE cached snapshot)
+The fork was bumped today fef0284→fdae51c = llama.cpp 2026-05-28 (release b9406). MTP IS implemented:
+- `--spec-type draft-mtp` in common/speculative.cpp (`COMMON_SPECULATIVE_TYPE_DRAFT_MTP`, `common_speculative_impl_draft_mtp`).
+- Core C API: `llama.h` adds `enum llama_context_type { DEFAULT, MTP }` + `llama_context_params.ctx_type`; `LLM_GRAPH_TYPE_DECODER_MTP`; MTP hook batches carry token + h_pre_norm embd row; `qwen35moe.cpp` builds the NextN/MTP block.
+- unsloth card flags (llama-server): `--spec-type draft-mtp --spec-draft-n-max 2 -np 1 -fa on`; ~1.5–2x faster; recommends UD-Q4_K_XL quant.

-Implication: pointing at the MTP-GGUF downloads larger files for ZERO runtime benefit today (the MTP head is loaded then skipped). Real MTP/speculative speedup requires implementing draft-model speculative decoding in our generation loop (+ possibly upstream MTP self-spec) — a substantial feature, not a flag.
+## WHY IT'S NOT A FLAG FOR US
+We embed libllama via llama-cpp-2 and run our own decode loop (GenerationHelper) — we do NOT run llama-server, so CLI flags don't apply. `common_speculative_*` is C++ (not the C API), so we must reimplement the orchestration on the core API. The high-level `llama-cpp-2` wrapper exposes none of the needed surface (no ctx_type, no set_embeddings_pre_norm, no embd+token batch, no hidden-state read, no set_sampler, no recurrent-state rollback).

-## Plan
-1. Consolidation (doable now): merge to a single `qwen` model (keep `kanban` tag), delete qwen-moe.yaml. Source choice pending user (MTP repo as requested = future-proof but inert/larger; vs regular Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF MXFP4_MOE = leaner, identical today).
-2. "Enable MTP": NOT possible via config. Filed as separate effort (speculative decoding implementation) — see follow-up.
\ No newline at end of file
+## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (draft-mtp in-process)
+1. FORK (github.com/swissarmyhammer/llama-cpp-rs — separate repo) — expose in llama-cpp-2:
+   - LlamaContextParams::with_ctx_type(LLAMA_CONTEXT_TYPE_MTP)
+   - llama_set_embeddings_pre_norm(ctx, enabled, masked)
+   - batch with BOTH token + embd rows (MTP hook batch)
+   - read hidden/pre-norm embd rows (llama_get_embeddings*)
+   - optional llama_set_sampler (backend draft sampling) — can start CPU-only
+   - recurrent/memory state save+restore for accept/reject rollback
+2. THIS REPO — speculative decode path mirroring common_speculative_impl_draft_mtp:
+   - create target ctx (default) + draft ctx (ctx_type=MTP) for the same model
+   - per step: MTP head proposes n_max draft tokens (carry h_pre_norm across batches) → batch-verify in target → accept longest matching prefix → roll back draft recurrent state past rejected drafts
+   - integrate with our KV-cache reuse + streaming chunking
+3. CONFIG (NO env vars): model-YAML field e.g. `spec: { draft_mtp: { n_max: 2 } }` → ModelConfig → context creation → enable the spec path only when set & model has nextn layers.
+4. VERIFY on Metal: real-model test asserting (a) identical output vs non-spec greedy, (b) acceptance>0 / fewer target forward passes than tokens.
+
+Scope: multi-step, spans the external fork repo + our generation loop. Not a quick edit.
\ No newline at end of file

-- unsloth card flags (llama-server): `--spec-type draft-mtp --spec-draft-n-max 2 -np 1 -fa on`; ~1.5–2x faster; recommends UD-Q4_K_XL quant.
-

-We embed libllama via llama-cpp-2 and run our own decode loop (GenerationHelper) — we do NOT run llama-server, so CLI flags don't apply. `common_speculative_*` is C++ (not the C API), so we must reimplement the orchestration on the core API. The high-level `llama-cpp-2` wrapper exposes none of the needed surface (no ctx_type, no set_embeddings_pre_norm, no embd+token batch, no hidden-state read, no set_sampler, no recurrent-state rollback).
+## RESEARCH FINDING (2026-05-29): MTP cannot be "enabled" via config in the current stack.
+Verified against the vendored llama.cpp (swissarmyhammer/llama-cpp-rs fork, llama.cpp snapshot ~2026-04, commit b8635075f):
+- The `qwen35moe` arch IS supported (src/models/qwen35moe.cpp) — the model loads & runs.
+- NextN/MTP support is LOAD-ONLY: the loader reads `nextn_predict_layers` and sets `n_layer_kv_from_start = n_layer - nextn_predict_layers`; the per-arch graph builders SKIP the NextN layer ("Only process up to last layer (skip final NextN layer)"). qwen35moe.cpp's graph does NOT reference nextn/mtp at all.
+- There is NO MTP self-speculative decode in the core path: no mtp/nextn in llama-context.cpp / llama-graph.cpp, and NO mtp/draft/speculative symbols in the public `include/llama.h` or the Rust bindings (llama-cpp-2 / llama-cpp-sys-2).

-## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (draft-mtp in-process)
-1. FORK (github.com/swissarmyhammer/llama-cpp-rs — separate repo) — expose in llama-cpp-2:
-   - LlamaContextParams::with_ctx_type(LLAMA_CONTEXT_TYPE_MTP)
-   - llama_set_embeddings_pre_norm(ctx, enabled, masked)
-   - batch with BOTH token + embd rows (MTP hook batch)
-   - read hidden/pre-norm embd rows (llama_get_embeddings*)
-   - optional llama_set_sampler (backend draft sampling) — can start CPU-only
-   - recurrent/memory state save+restore for accept/reject rollback
-2. THIS REPO — speculative decode path mirroring common_speculative_impl_draft_mtp:
-   - create target ctx (default) + draft ctx (ctx_type=MTP) for the same model
-   - per step: MTP head proposes n_max draft tokens (carry h_pre_norm across batches) → batch-verify in target → accept longest matching prefix → roll back draft recurrent state past rejected drafts
-   - integrate with our KV-cache reuse + streaming chunking
-3. CONFIG (NO env vars): model-YAML field e.g. `spec: { draft_mtp: { n_max: 2 } }` → ModelConfig → context creation → enable the spec path only when set & model has nextn layers.
-4. VERIFY on Metal: real-model test asserting (a) identical output vs non-spec greedy, (b) acceptance>0 / fewer target forward passes than tokens.

Real MTP/speculative speedup requires implementing draft-model speculative decoding in our generation loop (+ possibly upstream MTP self-spec) — a substantial feature, not a flag.

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,33 +5,10 @@

 ---
-Goal: one qwen model on Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP + MTP (Multi-Token Prediction) speculative decode enabled.
-
-## DONE

The high-level `llama-cpp-2` wrapper exposes none of the needed surface (no ctx_type, no set_embeddings_pre_norm, no embd+token batch, no hidden-state read, no set_sampler, no recurrent-state rollback).
+## STATUS (2026-05-29)
+Runtime is consolidated onto the NON-MTP Qwen3.6 MoE for now; MTP is the in-flight bindings effort.

-## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (draft-mtp in-process)
-1. FORK (github.com/swissarmyhammer/llama-cpp-rs — separate repo) — expose in llama-cpp-2:
-   - LlamaContextParams::with_ctx_type(LLAMA_CONTEXT_TYPE_MTP)
-   - llama_set_embeddings_pre_norm(ctx, enabled, masked)
-   - batch with BOTH token + embd rows (MTP hook batch)

-   - per step: MTP head proposes n_max draft tokens (carry h_pre_norm across batches) → batch-verify in target → accept longest matching prefix → roll back draft recurrent state past rejected drafts
-   - integrate with our KV-cache reuse + streaming chunking
-3. CONFIG (NO env vars): model-YAML field e.g. `spec: { draft_mtp: { n_max: 2 } }` → ModelConfig → context creation → enable the spec path only when set & model has nextn layers.
-4. VERIFY on Metal: real-model test asserting (a) identical output vs non-spec greedy, (b) acceptance>0 / fewer target forward passes than tokens.
+- Commit e3607f2bd: collapsed qwen + qwen-moe + qwen-coder into a SINGLE kanban-tagged `qwen` model on unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF / Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf (non-MTP). Deleted qwen-coder.yaml + qwen-moe.yaml; repointed all scattered refs (CLI help/reference, validators, config+CLI tests, UI mocks, embedder). Verified green.

@@ -5,10 +5,33 @@
 position_ordinal: '80'
 title: Consolidate qwen + qwen-moe onto Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP and enable MTP
 ---
-## STATUS (2026-05-29)
-Runtime is consolidated onto the NON-MTP Qwen3.6 MoE for now; MTP is the in-flight bindings effort.
+Goal: one qwen model on Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP + MTP (Multi-Token Prediction) speculative decode enabled.

-- Commit e3607f2bd: collapsed qwen + qwen-moe + qwen-coder into a SINGLE kanban-tagged `qwen` model on unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF / Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf (non-MTP). Deleted qwen-coder.yaml + qwen-moe.yaml; repointed all scattered refs (CLI help/reference, validators, config+CLI tests, UI mocks, embedder). Verified green.
-- MTP enablement: spec written at ../llama-cpp-rs/mtp.md (fork working copy). llama.cpp b9406 (submodule da3f990a4) HAS MTP: `--spec-type draft-mtp` + core `LLAMA_CONTEXT_TYPE_MTP`. Our gap is purely Rust bindings + an in-process draft→verify loop (we don't run llama-server). See mtp.md for the ~6 bindings to add in the fork (wrapper.h must include a C shim for src/llama-ext.h pre-norm fns; with_ctx_type; embd batch; pre-norm read; set_sampler; seq state) and the consumer-side orchestration plan.
+## DONE
+- Consolidated qwen + qwen-moe → single kanban-tagged `qwen` on unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MTP-GGUF / Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MXFP4_MOE.gguf; deleted qwen-moe.yaml; fixed the 2 model-enumeration tests. Commit 305b63d46. Runs as a normal MoE today (MTP head carried but not yet driven).

-NEXT for MTP (not started): implement fork bindings per mtp.md → implement draft-mtp loop in GenerationHelper gated by a model-YAML field → switch a qwen variant to the MTP GGUF → verify on Metal (identical-to-greedy output + acceptance>0).
\ No newline at end of file
+## CORRECTED FINDING (initial reading was the STALE cached snapshot)
+The fork was bumped today fef0284→fdae51c = llama.cpp 2026-05-28 (release b9406). MTP IS implemented:
+- `--spec-type draft-mtp` in common/speculative.cpp (`COMMON_SPECULATIVE_TYPE_DRAFT_MTP`, `common_speculative_impl_draft_mtp`).
+- Core C API: `llama.h` adds `enum llama_context_type { DEFAULT, MTP }` + `llama_context_params.ctx_type`; `LLM_GRAPH_TYPE_DECODER_MTP`; MTP hook batches carry token + h_pre_norm embd row; `qwen35moe.cpp` builds the NextN/MTP block.
+- unsloth card flags (llama-server): `--spec-type draft-mtp --spec-draft-n-max 2 -np 1 -fa on`; ~1.5–2x faster; recommends UD-Q4_K_XL quant.
+
+## WHY IT'S NOT A FLAG FOR US
+We embed libllama via llama-cpp-2 and run our own decode loop (GenerationHelper) — we do NOT run llama-server, so CLI flags don't apply. `common_speculative_*` is C++ (not the C API), so we must reimplement the orchestration on the core API. The high-level `llama-cpp-2` wrapper exposes none of the needed surface (no ctx_type, no set_embeddings_pre_norm, no embd+token batch, no hidden-state read, no set_sampler, no recurrent-state rollback).
+
+## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (draft-mtp in-process)
+1. FORK (github.com/swissarmyhammer/llama-cpp-rs — separate repo) — expose in llama-cpp-2:
+   - LlamaContextParams::with_ctx_type(LLAMA_CONTEXT_TYPE_MTP)
+   - llama_set_embeddings_pre_norm(ctx, enabled, masked)
+   - batch with BOTH token + embd rows (MTP hook batch)
+   - read hidden/pre-norm embd rows (llama_get_embeddings*)
+   - optional llama_set_sampler (backend draft sampling) — can start CPU-only
+   - recurrent/memory state save+restore for accept/reject rollback
+2. THIS REPO — speculative decode path mirroring common_speculative_impl_draft_mtp:
+   - create target ctx (default) + draft ctx (ctx_type=MTP) for the same model
+   - per step: MTP head proposes n_max draft tokens (carry h_pre_norm across batches) → batch-verify in target → accept longest matching prefix → roll back draft recurrent state past rejected drafts
+   - integrate with our KV-cache reuse + streaming chunking
+3. CONFIG (NO env vars): model-YAML field e.g. `spec: { draft_mtp: { n_max: 2 } }` → ModelConfig → context creation → enable the spec path only when set & model has nextn layers.
+4. VERIFY on Metal: real-model test asserting (a) identical output vs non-spec greedy, (b) acceptance>0 / fewer target forward passes than tokens.
+
+Scope: multi-step, spans the external fork repo + our generation loop. Not a quick edit.
\ No newline at end of file
